59#pragma once
60
61#include <string>
62#include <vector>
63#include <map>
64#include <memory>
65#include <initializer_list>
66
67#define JSON11_IS_DEFINED
68
69#ifdef _MSC_VER
70#if _MSC_VER <= 1800 // VS 2013
71 #ifndef noexcept
72 #define noexcept throw()
73 #endif
74
75 #ifndef snprintf
76 #define snprintf _snprintf_s
77 #endif
78 #endif
79#endif
80
81namespace json11 {
82
83 enum JsonParse {
84 STANDARD, COMMENTS
85 };
86
87 class JsonValue;
88
89 class Json final {
90 public:
91 // Types
92 enum Type {
93 NUL, NUMBER, BOOL, STRING, ARRAY, OBJECT
94 };
95
96 // Array and object typedefs
97 typedef std::vector<Json> array;
98 typedef std::map<std::string, Json> object;
99
100 // Constructors for the various types of JSON value.
101 inline Json() noexcept; // NUL
102 inline Json(std::nullptr_t) noexcept; // NUL
103 inline Json(double value); // NUMBER
104 inline Json(int value); // NUMBER
105 inline Json(bool value); // BOOL
106 inline Json(const std::string &value); // STRING
107 inline Json(std::string &&value); // STRING
108 inline Json(const char * value); // STRING
109 inline Json(const array &values); // ARRAY
110 inline Json(array &&values); // ARRAY
111 inline Json(const object &values); // OBJECT
112 inline Json(object &&values); // OBJECT
113
114 // Implicit constructor: anything with a to_json() function.
115 template <class T, class = decltype(&T::to_json)>
116 inline Json(const T & t) : Json(t.to_json()) {}
117
118 // Implicit constructor: map-like objects (std::map, std::unordered_map, etc)
119 template <class M, typename std::enable_if<
120 std::is_constructible<std::string, decltype(std::declval<M>().begin()->first)>::value
121 && std::is_constructible<Json, decltype(std::declval<M>().begin()->second)>::value,
122 int>::type = 0>
123 inline Json(const M & m) : Json(object(m.begin(), m.end())) {}
124
125 // Implicit constructor: vector-like objects (std::list, std::vector, std::set, etc)
126 template <class V, typename std::enable_if<
127 std::is_constructible<Json, decltype(*std::declval<V>().begin())>::value,
128 int>::type = 0>
129 inline Json(const V & v) : Json(array(v.begin(), v.end())) {}
130
131 // This prevents Json(some_pointer) from accidentally producing a bool. Use
132 // Json(bool(some_pointer)) if that behavior is desired.
133 Json(void *) = delete;
134
135 // Accessors
136 inline Type type() const;
137
138 inline bool is_null() const { return type() == NUL; }
139 inline bool is_number() const { return type() == NUMBER; }
140 inline bool is_bool() const { return type() == BOOL; }
141 inline bool is_string() const { return type() == STRING; }
142 inline bool is_array() const { return type() == ARRAY; }
143 inline bool is_object() const { return type() == OBJECT; }
144
145 // Return the enclosed value if this is a number, 0 otherwise. Note that json11 does not
146 // distinguish between integer and non-integer numbers - number_value() and int_value()
147 // can both be applied to a NUMBER-typed object.
148 inline double number_value() const;
149 inline int int_value() const;
150
151 // Return the enclosed value if this is a boolean, false otherwise.
152 inline bool bool_value() const;
153 // Return the enclosed string if this is a string, "" otherwise.
154 inline const std::string &string_value() const;
155 // Return the enclosed std::vector if this is an array, or an empty vector otherwise.
156 inline const array &array_items() const;
157 // Return the enclosed std::map if this is an object, or an empty map otherwise.
158 inline const object &object_items() const;
159
160 // Return a reference to arr[i] if this is an array, Json() otherwise.
161 inline const Json & operator[](size_t i) const;
162 // Return a reference to obj[key] if this is an object, Json() otherwise.
163 inline const Json & operator[](const std::string &key) const;
164
165 // Serialize.
166 inline void dump(std::string &out) const;
167 inline std::string dump() const {
168 std::string out;
169 dump(out);
170 return out;
171 }
172
173 // Parse. If parse fails, return Json() and assign an error message to err.
174 static inline Json parse(const std::string & in,
175 std::string & err,
176 JsonParse strategy = JsonParse::STANDARD);
177 static inline Json parse(const char * in,
178 std::string & err,
179 JsonParse strategy = JsonParse::STANDARD) {
180 if (in) {
181 return parse(std::string(in), err, strategy);
182 } else {
183 err = "null input";
184 return nullptr;
185 }
186 }
187 // Parse multiple objects, concatenated or separated by whitespace
188 static inline std::vector<Json> parse_multi(
189 const std::string & in,
190 std::string::size_type & parser_stop_pos,
191 std::string & err,
192 JsonParse strategy = JsonParse::STANDARD);
193
194 static inline std::vector<Json> parse_multi(
195 const std::string & in,
196 std::string & err,
197 JsonParse strategy = JsonParse::STANDARD) {
198 std::string::size_type parser_stop_pos;
199 return parse_multi(in, parser_stop_pos, err, strategy);
200 }
201
202 inline bool operator== (const Json &rhs) const;
203 inline bool operator< (const Json &rhs) const;
204 inline bool operator!= (const Json &rhs) const { return !(*this == rhs); }
205 inline bool operator<= (const Json &rhs) const { return !(rhs < *this); }
206 inline bool operator> (const Json &rhs) const { return (rhs < *this); }
207 inline bool operator>= (const Json &rhs) const { return !(*this < rhs); }
208
209 /* has_shape(types, err)
210 *
211 * Return true if this is a JSON object and, for each item in types, has a field of
212 * the given type. If not, return false and set err to a descriptive message.
213 */
214 typedef std::initializer_list<std::pair<std::string, Type>> shape;
215 inline bool has_shape(const shape & types, std::string & err) const;
216
217 private:
218 std::shared_ptr<JsonValue> m_ptr;
219 };
220
221// Internal class hierarchy - JsonValue objects are not exposed to users of this API.
222 class JsonValue {
223 protected:
224 friend class Json;
225 friend class JsonInt;
226 friend class JsonDouble;
227 virtual Json::Type type() const = 0;
228 virtual bool equals(const JsonValue * other) const = 0;
229 virtual bool less(const JsonValue * other) const = 0;
230 virtual void dump(std::string &out) const = 0;
231 virtual double number_value() const;
232 virtual int int_value() const;
233 virtual bool bool_value() const;
234 virtual const std::string &string_value() const;
235 virtual const Json::array &array_items() const;
236 virtual const Json &operator[](size_t i) const;
237 virtual const Json::object &object_items() const;
238 virtual const Json &operator[](const std::string &key) const;
239 virtual ~JsonValue() {}
240 };
241
242} // namespace json11
243

246#include <cassert>
247#include <cmath>
248#include <cstdlib>
249#include <cstdio>
250#include <limits>
251
252namespace json11 {
253
254 static const int max_depth = 200;
255
256 using std::string;
257 using std::vector;
258 using std::map;
259 using std::make_shared;
260 using std::initializer_list;
261 using std::move;
262
263/* Helper for representing null - just a do-nothing struct, plus comparison
264 * operators so the helpers in JsonValue work. We can't use nullptr_t because
265 * it may not be orderable.
266 */
267 struct NullStruct {
268 bool operator==(NullStruct) const { return true; }
269 bool operator<(NullStruct) const { return false; }
270 };
271
272/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
273 * Serialization
274 */
275
276 static void dump(NullStruct, string &out) {
277 out += "null";
278 }
279
280 static void dump(double value, string &out) {
281 if (std::isfinite(value)) {
282 char buf[32];
283 snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, "%.17g", value);
284 out += buf;
285 } else {
286 out += "null";
287 }
288 }
289
290 static void dump(int value, string &out) {
291 char buf[32];
292 snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, "%d", value);
293 out += buf;
294 }
295
296 static void dump(bool value, string &out) {
297 out += value ? "true" : "false";
298 }
299
300 static void dump(const string &value, string &out) {
301 out += '"';
302 for (size_t i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
303 const char ch = value[i];
304 if (ch == '\\') {
305 out += "\\\\";
306 } else if (ch == '"') {
307 out += "\\\"";
308 } else if (ch == '\b') {
309 out += "\\b";
310 } else if (ch == '\f') {
311 out += "\\f";
312 } else if (ch == '\n') {
313 out += "\\n";
314 } else if (ch == '\r') {
315 out += "\\r";
316 } else if (ch == '\t') {
317 out += "\\t";
318 } else if (static_cast<uint8_t>(ch) <= 0x1f) {
319 char buf[8];
320 snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, "\\u%04x", ch);
321 out += buf;
322 } else if (static_cast<uint8_t>(ch) == 0xe2 && static_cast<uint8_t>(value[i+1]) == 0x80
323 && static_cast<uint8_t>(value[i+2]) == 0xa8) {
324 out += "\\u2028";
325 i += 2;
326 } else if (static_cast<uint8_t>(ch) == 0xe2 && static_cast<uint8_t>(value[i+1]) == 0x80
327 && static_cast<uint8_t>(value[i+2]) == 0xa9) {
328 out += "\\u2029";
329 i += 2;
330 } else {
331 out += ch;
332 }
333 }
334 out += '"';
335 }
336
337 static void dump(const Json::array &values, string &out) {
338 bool first = true;
339 out += "[";
340 for (const auto &value : values) {
341 if (!first)
342 out += ", ";
343 value.dump(out);
344 first = false;
345 }
346 out += "]";
347 }
348
349 static void dump(const Json::object &values, string &out) {
350 bool first = true;
351 out += "{";
352 for (const auto &kv : values) {
353 if (!first)
354 out += ", ";
355 dump(kv.first, out);
356 out += ": ";
357 kv.second.dump(out);
358 first = false;
359 }
360 out += "}";
361 }
362
363 void Json::dump(string &out) const {
364 m_ptr->dump(out);
365 }
366
367/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
368 * Value wrappers
369 */
370
371 template <Json::Type tag, typename T>
372 class Value : public JsonValue {
373 protected:
374
375 // Constructors
376 explicit Value(const T &value) : m_value(value) {}
377 explicit Value(T &&value) : m_value(move(value)) {}
378
379 // Get type tag
380 Json::Type type() const override {
381 return tag;
382 }
383
384 // Comparisons
385 bool equals(const JsonValue * other) const override {
386 return m_value == static_cast<const Value<tag, T> *>(other)->m_value;
387 }
388 bool less(const JsonValue * other) const override {
389 return m_value < static_cast<const Value<tag, T> *>(other)->m_value;
390 }
391
392 const T m_value;
393 void dump(string &out) const override { json11::dump(m_value, out); }
394 };
395
396 class JsonDouble final : public Value<Json::NUMBER, double> {
397 double number_value() const override { return m_value; }
398 int int_value() const override { return static_cast<int>(m_value); }
399 bool equals(const JsonValue * other) const override { return m_value == other->number_value(); }
400 bool less(const JsonValue * other) const override { return m_value < other->number_value(); }
401 public:
402 explicit JsonDouble(double value) : Value(value) {}
403 };
404
405 class JsonInt final : public Value<Json::NUMBER, int> {
406 double number_value() const override { return m_value; }
407 int int_value() const override { return m_value; }
408 bool equals(const JsonValue * other) const override { return m_value == other->number_value(); }
409 bool less(const JsonValue * other) const override { return m_value < other->number_value(); }
410 public:
411 explicit JsonInt(int value) : Value(value) {}
412 };
413
414 class JsonBoolean final : public Value<Json::BOOL, bool> {
415 bool bool_value() const override { return m_value; }
416 public:
417 explicit JsonBoolean(bool value) : Value(value) {}
418 };
419
420 class JsonString final : public Value<Json::STRING, string> {
421 const string &string_value() const override { return m_value; }
422 public:
423 explicit JsonString(const string &value) : Value(value) {}
424 explicit JsonString(string &&value) : Value(move(value)) {}
425 };
426
427 class JsonArray final : public Value<Json::ARRAY, Json::array> {
428 const Json::array &array_items() const override { return m_value; }
429 const Json & operator[](size_t i) const override;
430 public:
431 explicit JsonArray(const Json::array &value) : Value(value) {}
432 explicit JsonArray(Json::array &&value) : Value(move(value)) {}
433 };
434
435 class JsonObject final : public Value<Json::OBJECT, Json::object> {
436 const Json::object &object_items() const override { return m_value; }
437 const Json & operator[](const string &key) const override;
438 public:
439 explicit JsonObject(const Json::object &value) : Value(value) {}
440 explicit JsonObject(Json::object &&value) : Value(move(value)) {}
441 };
442
443 class JsonNull final : public Value<Json::NUL, NullStruct> {
444 public:
445 JsonNull() : Value({}) {}
446 };
447
448/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
449 * Static globals - static-init-safe
450 */
451 struct Statics {
452 const std::shared_ptr<JsonValue> null = make_shared<JsonNull>();
453 const std::shared_ptr<JsonValue> t = make_shared<JsonBoolean>(true);
454 const std::shared_ptr<JsonValue> f = make_shared<JsonBoolean>(false);
455 const string empty_string;
456 const vector<Json> empty_vector;
457 const map<string, Json> empty_map;
458 Statics() {}
459 };
460
461 static const Statics & statics() {
462 static const Statics s {};
463 return s;
464 }
465
466 static const Json & static_null() {
467 // This has to be separate, not in Statics, because Json() accesses statics().null.
468 static const Json json_null;
469 return json_null;
470 }
471
472/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
473 * Constructors
474 */
475
476 Json::Json() noexcept : m_ptr(statics().null) {}
477 Json::Json(std::nullptr_t) noexcept : m_ptr(statics().null) {}
478 Json::Json(double value) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonDouble>(value)) {}
479 Json::Json(int value) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonInt>(value)) {}
480 Json::Json(bool value) : m_ptr(value ? statics().t : statics().f) {}
481 Json::Json(const string &value) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonString>(value)) {}
482 Json::Json(string &&value) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonString>(move(value))) {}
483 Json::Json(const char * value) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonString>(value)) {}
484 Json::Json(const Json::array &values) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonArray>(values)) {}
485 Json::Json(Json::array &&values) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonArray>(move(values))) {}
486 Json::Json(const Json::object &values) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonObject>(values)) {}
487 Json::Json(Json::object &&values) : m_ptr(make_shared<JsonObject>(move(values))) {}
488
489/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
490 * Accessors
491 */
492
493 inline Json::Type Json::type() const { return m_ptr->type(); }
494 inline double Json::number_value() const { return m_ptr->number_value(); }
495 inline int Json::int_value() const { return m_ptr->int_value(); }
496 inline bool Json::bool_value() const { return m_ptr->bool_value(); }
497 inline const string & Json::string_value() const { return m_ptr->string_value(); }
498 inline const vector<Json> & Json::array_items() const { return m_ptr->array_items(); }
499 inline const map<string, Json> & Json::object_items() const { return m_ptr->object_items(); }
500 inline const Json & Json::operator[] (size_t i) const { return (*m_ptr)[i]; }
501 inline const Json & Json::operator[] (const string &key) const { return (*m_ptr)[key]; }
502
503 inline double JsonValue::number_value() const { return 0; }
504 inline int JsonValue::int_value() const { return 0; }
505 inline bool JsonValue::bool_value() const { return false; }
506 inline const string & JsonValue::string_value() const { return statics().empty_string; }
507 inline const vector<Json> & JsonValue::array_items() const { return statics().empty_vector; }
508 inline const map<string, Json> & JsonValue::object_items() const { return statics().empty_map; }
509 inline const Json & JsonValue::operator[] (size_t) const { return static_null(); }
510 inline const Json & JsonValue::operator[] (const string &) const { return static_null(); }
511
512 inline const Json & JsonObject::operator[] (const string &key) const {
513 auto iter = m_value.find(key);
514 return (iter == m_value.end()) ? static_null() : iter->second;
515 }
516 inline const Json & JsonArray::operator[] (size_t i) const {
517 if (i >= m_value.size()) return static_null();
518 else return m_value[i];
519 }
520
521/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
522 * Comparison
523 */
524
525 bool Json::operator== (const Json &other) const {
526 if (m_ptr == other.m_ptr)
527 return true;
528 if (m_ptr->type() != other.m_ptr->type())
529 return false;
530
531 return m_ptr->equals(other.m_ptr.get());
532 }
533
534 bool Json::operator< (const Json &other) const {
535 if (m_ptr == other.m_ptr)
536 return false;
537 if (m_ptr->type() != other.m_ptr->type())
538 return m_ptr->type() < other.m_ptr->type();
539
540 return m_ptr->less(other.m_ptr.get());
541 }
542
543/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
544 * Parsing
545 */
546
547/* esc(c)
548 *
549 * Format char c suitable for printing in an error message.
550 */
551 static inline string esc(char c) {
552 char buf[12];
553 if (static_cast<uint8_t>(c) >= 0x20 && static_cast<uint8_t>(c) <= 0x7f) {
554 snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, "'%c' (%d)", c, c);
555 } else {
556 snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, "(%d)", c);
557 }
558 return string(buf);
559 }
560
561 static inline bool in_range(long x, long lower, long upper) {
562 return (x >= lower && x <= upper);
563 }
564
565 namespace {
566/* JsonParser
567 *
568 * Object that tracks all state of an in-progress parse.
569 */
570 struct JsonParser final {
571
572 /* State
573 */
574 const string &str;
575 size_t i;
576 string &err;
577 bool failed;
578 const JsonParse strategy;
579
580 /* fail(msg, err_ret = Json())
581 *
582 * Mark this parse as failed.
583 */
584 Json fail(string &&msg) {
585 return fail(move(msg), Json());
586 }
587
588 template <typename T>
589 T fail(string &&msg, const T err_ret) {
590 if (!failed)
591 err = std::move(msg);
592 failed = true;
593 return err_ret;
594 }
595
596 /* consume_whitespace()
597 *
598 * Advance until the current character is non-whitespace.
599 */
600 void consume_whitespace() {
601 while (str[i] == ' ' || str[i] == '\r' || str[i] == '\n' || str[i] == '\t')
602 i++;
603 }
604
605 /* consume_comment()
606 *
607 * Advance comments (c-style inline and multiline).
608 */
609 bool consume_comment() {
610 bool comment_found = false;
611 if (str[i] == '/') {
612 i++;
613 if (i == str.size())
614 return fail("unexpected end of input after start of comment", false);
615 if (str[i] == '/') { // inline comment
616 i++;
617 // advance until next line, or end of input
618 while (i < str.size() && str[i] != '\n') {
619 i++;
620 }
621 comment_found = true;
622 }
623 else if (str[i] == '*') { // multiline comment
624 i++;
625 if (i > str.size()-2)
626 return fail("unexpected end of input inside multi-line comment", false);
627 // advance until closing tokens
628 while (!(str[i] == '*' && str[i+1] == '/')) {
629 i++;
630 if (i > str.size()-2)
631 return fail(
632 "unexpected end of input inside multi-line comment", false);
633 }
634 i += 2;
635 comment_found = true;
636 }
637 else
638 return fail("malformed comment", false);
639 }
640 return comment_found;
641 }
642
643 /* consume_garbage()
644 *
645 * Advance until the current character is non-whitespace and non-comment.
646 */
647 void consume_garbage() {
648 consume_whitespace();
649 if(strategy == JsonParse::COMMENTS) {
650 bool comment_found = false;
651 do {
652 comment_found = consume_comment();
653 if (failed) return;
654 consume_whitespace();
655 }
656 while(comment_found);
657 }
658 }
659
660 /* get_next_token()
661 *
662 * Return the next non-whitespace character. If the end of the input is reached,
663 * flag an error and return 0.
664 */
665 char get_next_token() {
666 consume_garbage();
667 if (failed) return static_cast<char>(0);
668 if (i == str.size())
669 return fail("unexpected end of input", static_cast<char>(0));
670
671 return str[i++];
672 }
673
674 /* encode_utf8(pt, out)
675 *
676 * Encode pt as UTF-8 and add it to out.
677 */
678 void encode_utf8(long pt, string & out) {
679 if (pt < 0)
680 return;
681
682 if (pt < 0x80) {
683 out += static_cast<char>(pt);
684 } else if (pt < 0x800) {
685 out += static_cast<char>((pt >> 6) | 0xC0);
686 out += static_cast<char>((pt & 0x3F) | 0x80);
687 } else if (pt < 0x10000) {
688 out += static_cast<char>((pt >> 12) | 0xE0);
689 out += static_cast<char>(((pt >> 6) & 0x3F) | 0x80);
690 out += static_cast<char>((pt & 0x3F) | 0x80);
691 } else {
692 out += static_cast<char>((pt >> 18) | 0xF0);
693 out += static_cast<char>(((pt >> 12) & 0x3F) | 0x80);
694 out += static_cast<char>(((pt >> 6) & 0x3F) | 0x80);
695 out += static_cast<char>((pt & 0x3F) | 0x80);
696 }
697 }
698
699 /* parse_string()
700 *
701 * Parse a string, starting at the current position.
702 */
703 string parse_string() {
704 string out;
705 long last_escaped_codepoint = -1;
706 while (true) {
707 if (i == str.size())
708 return fail("unexpected end of input in string", "");
709
710 char ch = str[i++];
711
712 if (ch == '"') {
713 encode_utf8(last_escaped_codepoint, out);
714 return out;
715 }
716
717 if (in_range(ch, 0, 0x1f))
718 return fail("unescaped " + esc(ch) + " in string", "");
719
720 // The usual case: non-escaped characters
721 if (ch != '\\') {
722 encode_utf8(last_escaped_codepoint, out);
723 last_escaped_codepoint = -1;
724 out += ch;
725 continue;
726 }
727
728 // Handle escapes
729 if (i == str.size())
730 return fail("unexpected end of input in string", "");
731
732 ch = str[i++];
733
734 if (ch == 'u') {
735 // Extract 4-byte escape sequence
736 string esc = str.substr(i, 4);
737 // Explicitly check length of the substring. The following loop
738 // relies on std::string returning the terminating NUL when
739 // accessing str[length]. Checking here reduces brittleness.
740 if (esc.length() < 4) {
741 return fail("bad \\u escape: " + esc, "");
742 }
743 for (size_t j = 0; j < 4; j++) {
744 if (!in_range(esc[j], 'a', 'f') && !in_range(esc[j], 'A', 'F')
745 && !in_range(esc[j], '0', '9'))
746 return fail("bad \\u escape: " + esc, "");
747 }
748
749 long codepoint = strtol(esc.data(), nullptr, 16);
750
751 // JSON specifies that characters outside the BMP shall be encoded as a pair
752 // of 4-hex-digit \u escapes encoding their surrogate pair components. Check
753 // whether we're in the middle of such a beast: the previous codepoint was an
754 // escaped lead (high) surrogate, and this is a trail (low) surrogate.
755 if (in_range(last_escaped_codepoint, 0xD800, 0xDBFF)
756 && in_range(codepoint, 0xDC00, 0xDFFF)) {
757 // Reassemble the two surrogate pairs into one astral-plane character, per
758 // the UTF-16 algorithm.
759 encode_utf8((((last_escaped_codepoint - 0xD800) << 10)
760 | (codepoint - 0xDC00)) + 0x10000, out);
761 last_escaped_codepoint = -1;
762 } else {
763 encode_utf8(last_escaped_codepoint, out);
764 last_escaped_codepoint = codepoint;
765 }
766
767 i += 4;
768 continue;
769 }
770
771 encode_utf8(last_escaped_codepoint, out);
772 last_escaped_codepoint = -1;
773
774 if (ch == 'b') {
775 out += '\b';
776 } else if (ch == 'f') {
777 out += '\f';
778 } else if (ch == 'n') {
779 out += '\n';
780 } else if (ch == 'r') {
781 out += '\r';
782 } else if (ch == 't') {
783 out += '\t';
784 } else if (ch == '"' || ch == '\\' || ch == '/') {
785 out += ch;
786 } else {
787 return fail("invalid escape character " + esc(ch), "");
788 }
789 }
790 }
791
792 /* parse_number()
793 *
794 * Parse a double.
795 */
796 Json parse_number() {
797 size_t start_pos = i;
798
799 if (str[i] == '-')
800 i++;
801
802 // Integer part
803 if (str[i] == '0') {
804 i++;
805 if (in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
806 return fail("leading 0s not permitted in numbers");
807 } else if (in_range(str[i], '1', '9')) {
808 i++;
809 while (in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
810 i++;
811 } else {
812 return fail("invalid " + esc(str[i]) + " in number");
813 }
814
815 if (str[i] != '.' && str[i] != 'e' && str[i] != 'E'
816 && (i - start_pos) <= static_cast<size_t>(std::numeric_limits<int>::digits10)) {
817 return std::atoi(str.c_str() + start_pos);
818 }
819
820 // Decimal part
821 if (str[i] == '.') {
822 i++;
823 if (!in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
824 return fail("at least one digit required in fractional part");
825
826 while (in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
827 i++;
828 }
829
830 // Exponent part
831 if (str[i] == 'e' || str[i] == 'E') {
832 i++;
833
834 if (str[i] == '+' || str[i] == '-')
835 i++;
836
837 if (!in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
838 return fail("at least one digit required in exponent");
839
840 while (in_range(str[i], '0', '9'))
841 i++;
842 }
843
844 return std::strtod(str.c_str() + start_pos, nullptr);
845 }
846
847 /* expect(str, res)
848 *
849 * Expect that 'str' starts at the character that was just read. If it does, advance
850 * the input and return res. If not, flag an error.
851 */
852 Json expect(const string &expected, Json res) {
853 assert(i != 0);
854 i--;
855 if (str.compare(i, expected.length(), expected) == 0) {
856 i += expected.length();
857 return res;
858 } else {
859 return fail("parse error: expected " + expected + ", got " + str.substr(i, expected.length()));
860 }
861 }
862
863 /* parse_json()
864 *
865 * Parse a JSON object.
866 */
867 Json parse_json(int depth) {
868 if (depth > max_depth) {
869 return fail("exceeded maximum nesting depth");
870 }
871
872 char ch = get_next_token();
873 if (failed)
874 return Json();
875
876 if (ch == '-' || (ch >= '0' && ch <= '9')) {
877 i--;
878 return parse_number();
879 }
880
881 if (ch == 't')
882 return expect("true", true);
883
884 if (ch == 'f')
885 return expect("false", false);
886
887 if (ch == 'n')
888 return expect("null", Json());
889
890 if (ch == '"')
891 return parse_string();
892
893 if (ch == '{') {
894 map<string, Json> data;
895 ch = get_next_token();
896 if (ch == '}')
897 return data;
898
899 while (1) {
900 if (ch != '"')
901 return fail("expected '\"' in object, got " + esc(ch));
902
903 string key = parse_string();
904 if (failed)
905 return Json();
906
907 ch = get_next_token();
908 if (ch != ':')
909 return fail("expected ':' in object, got " + esc(ch));
910
911 data[std::move(key)] = parse_json(depth + 1);
912 if (failed)
913 return Json();
914
915 ch = get_next_token();
916 if (ch == '}')
917 break;
918 if (ch != ',')
919 return fail("expected ',' in object, got " + esc(ch));
920
921 ch = get_next_token();
922 }
923 return data;
924 }
925
926 if (ch == '[') {
927 vector<Json> data;
928 ch = get_next_token();
929 if (ch == ']')
930 return data;
931
932 while (1) {
933 i--;
934 data.push_back(parse_json(depth + 1));
935 if (failed)
936 return Json();
937
938 ch = get_next_token();
939 if (ch == ']')
940 break;
941 if (ch != ',')
942 return fail("expected ',' in list, got " + esc(ch));
943
944 ch = get_next_token();
945 (void)ch;
946 }
947 return data;
948 }
949
950 return fail("expected value, got " + esc(ch));
951 }
952 };
953 }//namespace {
954
955 Json Json::parse(const string &in, string &err, JsonParse strategy) {
956 JsonParser parser { in, 0, err, false, strategy };
957 Json result = parser.parse_json(0);
958
959 // Check for any trailing garbage
960 parser.consume_garbage();
961 if (parser.failed)
962 return Json();
963 if (parser.i != in.size() &&
964 ((parser.i + 1) != in.size() && in[parser.i] != 0)) //RBP: If there is only 1 character diff, it is probably just a terminating zero from a memory read.
965 {
966 return parser.fail("unexpected trailing " + esc(in[parser.i]));
967 }
968 return result;
969 }
970
971// Documented in json11.hpp
972 vector<Json> Json::parse_multi(const string &in,
973 std::string::size_type &parser_stop_pos,
974 string &err,
975 JsonParse strategy) {
976 JsonParser parser { in, 0, err, false, strategy };
977 parser_stop_pos = 0;
978 vector<Json> json_vec;
979 while (parser.i != in.size() && !parser.failed) {
980 json_vec.push_back(parser.parse_json(0));
981 if (parser.failed)
982 break;
983
984 // Check for another object
985 parser.consume_garbage();
986 if (parser.failed)
987 break;
988 parser_stop_pos = parser.i;
989 }
990 return json_vec;
991 }
992
993/* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *
994 * Shape-checking
995 */
996
997 bool Json::has_shape(const shape & types, string & err) const {
998 if (!is_object()) {
999 err = "expected JSON object, got " + dump();
1000 return false;
1001 }
1002
1003 const auto& obj_items = object_items();
1004 for (auto & item : types) {
1005 const auto it = obj_items.find(item.first);
1006 if (it == obj_items.cend() || it->second.type() != item.second) {
1007 err = "bad type for " + item.first + " in " + dump();
1008 return false;
1009 }
1010 }
1011
1012 return true;
1013 }
1014
1015} // namespace json11
1016
1017/*** End of inlined file: json11.cpp ***/
1018
1019/*** End of inlined file: json11.hpp ***/
1020
1021

1025//

1027#ifndef TILESON_TILESON_PARSER_HPP
1028#define TILESON_TILESON_PARSER_HPP
1029
1030//RBP: FS-namespace is defined in tileson_parser now!
1031#if _MSC_VER && !__INTEL_COMPILER
1032 #include <filesystem>
1033 namespace fs = std::filesystem;
1034#elif __MINGW64__
1035 #if __MINGW64_VERSION_MAJOR > 6
1036 #include <filesystem>
1037 namespace fs = std::filesystem;
1038 #else
1039 #include <experimental/filesystem>
1040 namespace fs = std::experimental::filesystem;
1041 #endif
1042#elif __clang__
1043 #if __clang_major__ < 8
1044 #include <experimental/filesystem>
1045 namespace fs = std::experimental::filesystem;
1046 #else
1047 #include <filesystem>
1048 namespace fs = std::filesystem;
1049 #endif
1050#else //Linux
1051 #if __GNUC__ < 8 //GCC major version less than 8
1052 #include <experimental/filesystem>
1053 namespace fs = std::experimental::filesystem;
1054 #else
1055 #include <filesystem>
1056 namespace fs = std::filesystem;
1057 #endif
1058#endif
1059
1060#include <fstream>
1061#include <sstream>
1062#include <memory>
1063
1064
1065/*** Start of inlined file: Tools.hpp ***/
1066//
1067// Created by robin on 31.07.2020.
1068//
1069
1070#ifndef TILESON_TOOLS_HPP
1071#define TILESON_TOOLS_HPP
1072
1073#include <cstdint>
1074#include <vector>
1075#include <string_view>
1076namespace tson
1077{
1078 class Tools
1079 {
1080
1081 public:
1082 Tools() = delete;
1083 ~Tools() = delete;
1084 inline static std::vector<uint8_t> Base64DecodedStringToBytes(std::string_view str);
1085 inline static std::vector<uint32_t> BytesToUnsignedInts(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bytes);
1086 inline static std::vector<std::string> SplitString(const std::string &s, char delim);
1087 inline static bool Equal(float a, float b, float precision = 8192.f);
1088
1089 private:
1090 template<typename Out>
1091 static void split(const std::string &s, char delim, Out result)
1092 {
1093 std::stringstream ss;
1094 ss.str(s);
1095 std::string item;
1096
1097 while (std::getline(ss, item, delim))
1098 {
1099 *(result++) = item;
1100 }
1101 }
1102 };
1103
1109 std::vector<uint8_t> Tools::Base64DecodedStringToBytes(std::string_view str)
1110 {
1111 std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
1112 for(size_t i = 0; i < str.size(); ++i)
1113 {
1114 uint8_t u8 = static_cast<uint8_t>(str[i]);
1115 bytes.push_back(u8);
1116 }
1117 return bytes;
1118 }
1119
1125 std::vector<uint32_t> Tools::BytesToUnsignedInts(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bytes)
1126 {
1127 std::vector<uint32_t> uints;
1128 std::vector<uint8_t> toConvert;
1129 //uint32_t size8 = (compressed[55] << 24) | (compressed[56] << 16) | (compressed[57] << 8) | compressed[58]; //Should be 66000
1130
1131 for(size_t i = 0; i < bytes.size(); ++i)
1132 {
1133 toConvert.push_back(bytes[i]);
1134 if(toConvert.size() == 4)
1135 {
1136 uint32_t u32 = (toConvert[3] << 24) | (toConvert[2] << 16) | (toConvert[1] << 8) | toConvert[0];
1137 uints.push_back(u32);
1138 toConvert.clear();
1139 }
1140 }
1141
1142 return uints;
1143 }
1144
1145 std::vector<std::string> Tools::SplitString(const std::string &s, char delim)
1146 {
1147 std::vector<std::string> elems;
1148 split(s, delim, std::back_inserter(elems));
1149 return elems;
1150 }
1151
1158 bool Tools::Equal(float a, float b, float precision)
1159 {
1160 float threshold = 1.f / precision;
1161 float diff = fabsf(a - b);
1162 return diff <= threshold;
1163 }
1164}
1165
1166#endif //TILESON_TOOLS_HPP
1167
1168/*** End of inlined file: Tools.hpp ***/
1169
1170
1171/*** Start of inlined file: Base64Decompressor.hpp ***/
1172//
1173// Created by robin on 29.07.2020.
1174// The Base64 decoding logic is heavily based on: https://github.com/ReneNyffenegger/cpp-base64
1175//
1176
1177#ifndef TILESON_BASE64DECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1178#define TILESON_BASE64DECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1179
1180
1181/*** Start of inlined file: IDecompressor.hpp ***/
1182//
1183// Created by robin on 29.07.2020.
1184//
1185
1186#ifndef TILESON_IDECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1187#define TILESON_IDECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1188
1189#include <string_view>
1190
1191namespace tson
1192{
1193 template <class TIn, class TOut>
1194 class IDecompressor
1195 {
1196 public:
1208 [[nodiscard]] virtual const std::string &name() const = 0;
1209
1215 virtual TOut decompress(const TIn &input) = 0;
1216
1222 virtual TOut decompressFile(const fs::path &path) = 0;
1223
1229 virtual TOut decompress(const void *data, size_t size) = 0;
1230
1234 virtual ~IDecompressor() = default;
1235 };
1236}
1237
1238#endif //TILESON_IDECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1239
1240/*** End of inlined file: IDecompressor.hpp ***/
1241
1242#include <string>
1243
1244namespace tson
1245{
1246 class Base64Decompressor : public IDecompressor<std::string_view, std::string>
1247 {
1248 public:
1249 [[nodiscard]] inline const std::string &name() const override;
1250
1251 inline std::string decompress(const std::string_view &s) override;
1252
1253 inline std::string decompressFile(const fs::path &path) override;
1254 inline std::string decompress(const void *data, size_t size) override;
1255
1256 private:
1257 inline unsigned int pos_of_char(const unsigned char chr);
1258 inline static const std::string NAME = "base64";
1259 };
1260
1261 const std::string &Base64Decompressor::name() const
1262 {
1263 return NAME;
1264 }
1265
1266 std::string Base64Decompressor::decompress(const std::string_view &s)
1267 {
1268
1269 size_t length_of_string = s.length();
1270 if (!length_of_string) return std::string("");
1271
1272 size_t in_len = length_of_string;
1273 size_t pos = 0;
1274
1275 //
1276 // The approximate length (bytes) of the decoded string might be one ore
1277 // two bytes smaller, depending on the amount of trailing equal signs
1278 // in the encoded string. This approximation is needed to reserve
1279 // enough space in the string to be returned.
1280 //
1281 size_t approx_length_of_decoded_string = length_of_string / 4 * 3;
1282 std::string ret;
1283 ret.reserve(approx_length_of_decoded_string);
1284
1285 while (pos < in_len) {
1286
1287 unsigned int pos_of_char_1 = pos_of_char(s[pos+1] );
1288
1289 ret.push_back(static_cast<std::string::value_type>( ( (pos_of_char(s[pos+0]) ) << 2 ) + ( (pos_of_char_1 & 0x30 ) >> 4)));
1290
1291 if (s[pos+2] != '=' && s[pos+2] != '.') { // accept URL-safe base 64 strings, too, so check for '.' also.
1292
1293 unsigned int pos_of_char_2 = pos_of_char(s[pos+2] );
1294 ret.push_back(static_cast<std::string::value_type>( (( pos_of_char_1 & 0x0f) << 4) + (( pos_of_char_2 & 0x3c) >> 2)));
1295
1296 if (s[pos+3] != '=' && s[pos+3] != '.') {
1297 ret.push_back(static_cast<std::string::value_type>( ( (pos_of_char_2 & 0x03 ) << 6 ) + pos_of_char(s[pos+3]) ));
1298 }
1299 }
1300
1301 pos += 4;
1302 }
1303
1304 return ret;
1305 }
1306
1307 unsigned int Base64Decompressor::pos_of_char(const unsigned char chr)
1308 {
1309 //
1310 // Return the position of chr within base64_encode()
1311 //
1312
1313 if (chr >= 'A' && chr <= 'Z') return chr - 'A';
1314 else if (chr >= 'a' && chr <= 'z') return chr - 'a' + ('Z' - 'A') + 1;
1315 else if (chr >= '0' && chr <= '9') return chr - '0' + ('Z' - 'A') + ('z' - 'a') + 2;
1316 else if (chr == '+' || chr == '-') return 62; // Be liberal with input and accept both url ('-') and non-url ('+') base 64 characters (
1317 else if (chr == '/' || chr == '_') return 63; // Ditto for '/' and '_'
1318
1319 throw "If input is correct, this line should never be reached.";
1320 }
1321
1327 std::string Base64Decompressor::decompressFile(const fs::path &path)
1328 {
1329 return std::string();
1330 }
1331
1337 std::string Base64Decompressor::decompress(const void *data, size_t size)
1338 {
1339 return std::string();
1340 }
1341}
1342
1343#endif //TILESON_BASE64DECOMPRESSOR_HPP
1344
1345/*** End of inlined file: Base64Decompressor.hpp ***/
1346
1347
1348/*** Start of inlined file: Lzma.hpp ***/
1349//
1350// Created by robin on 16.01.2021.
1351//
1352//#include "../../extras/pocketlzma.hpp"
1353#ifdef POCKETLZMA_POCKETLZMA_H
1354
1355#ifndef TILESON_LZMA_HPP
1356#define TILESON_LZMA_HPP
1357
1358namespace tson
1359{
1360 class Lzma : public IDecompressor<std::vector<uint8_t>, std::vector<uint8_t>>
1361 {
1362 public:
1363 inline const std::string &name() const override
1364 {
1365 return NAME;
1366 }
1367
1368 inline std::vector<uint8_t> decompress(const std::vector<uint8_t> &input) override
1369 {
1370 std::vector<uint8_t> out;
1371
1372 plz::PocketLzma p;
1373 plz::StatusCode status = p.decompress(input, out);
1374
1375 if(status != plz::StatusCode::Ok)
1376 return std::vector<uint8_t>();
1377
1378 return out;
1379 }
1380
1381 inline std::vector<uint8_t> decompressFile(const fs::path &path) override
1382 {
1383 std::vector<uint8_t> in;
1384 std::vector<uint8_t> out;
1385
1386 plz::PocketLzma p;
1387 plz::FileStatus fileStatus = plz::File::FromFile(path.generic_string(), in);
1388 if(fileStatus.status() != plz::FileStatus::Code::Ok)
1389 return std::vector<uint8_t>();
1390
1391 plz::StatusCode status = p.decompress(in, out);
1392
1393 if(status != plz::StatusCode::Ok)
1394 return std::vector<uint8_t>();
1395
1396 return out;
1397 }
1398
1399 inline std::vector<uint8_t> decompress(const void *data, size_t size) override
1400 {
1401 std::vector<uint8_t> out;
1402
1403 plz::PocketLzma p;
1404 plz::StatusCode status = p.decompress((uint8_t*) data, size, out);
1405
1406 if(status != plz::StatusCode::Ok)
1407 return std::vector<uint8_t>();
1408
1409 return out;
1410 }
1411
1412 private:
1413 inline static const std::string NAME {"lzma"};
1414 };
1415}
1416
1417#endif //TILESON_LZMA_HPP
1418
1419#endif
1420/*** End of inlined file: Lzma.hpp ***/
1421
1422
1423/*** Start of inlined file: DecompressorContainer.hpp ***/
1424//
1425// Created by robin on 30.07.2020.
1426//
1427
1428#ifndef TILESON_DECOMPRESSORCONTAINER_HPP
1429#define TILESON_DECOMPRESSORCONTAINER_HPP
1430
1431#include <memory>
1432#include <vector>
1433#include <string_view>
1434#include <functional>
1435namespace tson
1436{
1437 class DecompressorContainer
1438 {
1439 public:
1440 inline DecompressorContainer() = default;
1441 template <typename T, typename... Args>
1442 inline void add(Args &&... args);
1443 inline void remove(std::string_view name);
1444 inline bool contains(std::string_view name) const;
1445 inline bool empty() const;
1446 inline size_t size() const;
1447 inline void clear();
1448
1449 inline IDecompressor<std::string_view, std::string> *get(std::string_view name);
1450 private:
1451 //Key: name,
1452 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IDecompressor<std::string_view, std::string>>> m_decompressors;
1453 };
1454
1455 template<typename T, typename... Args>
1456 void DecompressorContainer::add(Args &&... args)
1457 {
1458 m_decompressors.emplace_back(new T(args...));
1459 }
1460
1466 bool DecompressorContainer::contains(std::string_view name) const
1467 {
1468 auto iter = std::find_if(m_decompressors.begin(), m_decompressors.end(), [&](const auto &item)
1469 {
1470 return item->name() == name;
1471 });
1472
1473 return iter != m_decompressors.end();
1474 }
1475
1480 void DecompressorContainer::remove(std::string_view name)
1481 {
1482 auto iter = std::remove_if(m_decompressors.begin(), m_decompressors.end(), [&](const auto &item)
1483 {
1484 return item->name() == name;
1485 });
1486 m_decompressors.erase(iter);
1487 }
1488
1489 size_t DecompressorContainer::size() const
1490 {
1491 return m_decompressors.size();
1492 }
1493
1499 IDecompressor<std::string_view, std::string> *DecompressorContainer::get(std::string_view name)
1500 {
1501 auto iter = std::find_if(m_decompressors.begin(), m_decompressors.end(), [&](const auto &item)
1502 {
1503 return item->name() == name;
1504 });
1505
1506 return (iter != m_decompressors.end()) ? iter->get() : nullptr;
1507 }
1508
1513 bool DecompressorContainer::empty() const
1514 {
1515 return m_decompressors.empty();
1516 }
1517
1521 void DecompressorContainer::clear()
1522 {
1523 m_decompressors.clear();
1524 }
1525}
1526#endif //TILESON_DECOMPRESSORCONTAINER_HPP
1527
1528/*** End of inlined file: DecompressorContainer.hpp ***/
1529
1530
1531/*** Start of inlined file: MemoryStream.hpp ***/
1532//
1533// Created by robin on 22.03.2020.
1534//
1535
1536#ifndef TILESON_MEMORYSTREAM_HPP
1537#define TILESON_MEMORYSTREAM_HPP
1538
1539
1540/*** Start of inlined file: MemoryBuffer.hpp ***/
1541//
1542// Created by robin on 22.03.2020.
1543//
1544
1545#ifndef TILESON_MEMORYBUFFER_HPP
1546#define TILESON_MEMORYBUFFER_HPP
1547
1548#include <iostream>
1549
1550namespace tson
1551{
1552 class MemoryBuffer : public std::basic_streambuf<char> {
1553 public:
1554 MemoryBuffer(const uint8_t *p, size_t l) {
1555 setg((char*)p, (char*)p, (char*)p + l);
1556 }
1557 };
1558}
1559
1560#endif //TILESON_MEMORYBUFFER_HPP
1561
1562/*** End of inlined file: MemoryBuffer.hpp ***/
1563
1564namespace tson
1565{
1566 class MemoryStream : public std::istream {
1567 public:
1568 MemoryStream(const uint8_t *p, size_t l) :
1569 std::istream(&m_buffer),
1570 m_buffer(p, l) {
1571 rdbuf(&m_buffer);
1572 }
1573
1574 private:
1575 MemoryBuffer m_buffer;
1576 };
1577}
1578
1579#endif //TILESON_MEMORYSTREAM_HPP
1580
1581/*** End of inlined file: MemoryStream.hpp ***/
1582
1583
1584/*** Start of inlined file: Map.hpp ***/
1585//
1586// Created by robin on 22.03.2020.
1587//
1588
1589#ifndef TILESON_MAP_HPP
1590#define TILESON_MAP_HPP
1591
1592
1593/*** Start of inlined file: Color.hpp ***/
1594//
1595// Created by robin on 09.08.2019.
1596//
1597
1598#ifndef TILESON_COLOR_HPP
1599#define TILESON_COLOR_HPP
1600
1601#include <type_traits>
1602#include <cstdint>
1603#include <string>
1604
1605namespace tson
1606{
1607
1608 template<typename T>
1609 class Color
1610 {
1611
1612 public:
1618 inline explicit Color(const std::string &color)
1619 {
1620 parseHexString(color);
1621 }
1622 inline Color(T red, T green, T blue, T alpha);
1623 inline Color() { r = g = b = 0; a = 255; }
1624
1625 inline bool operator==(const Color &rhs) const;
1626 inline bool operator==(const std::string &rhs) const;
1627 inline bool operator!=(const Color &rhs) const;
1628
1629 inline Color<float> asFloat();
1630 inline Color<uint8_t> asInt();
1631
1633 T r;
1635 T g;
1637 T b;
1639 T a;
1640
1641 private:
1642 void parseHexString(const std::string &color)
1643 {
1644 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, float>::value)
1645 {
1646 if (color.size() == 9)
1647 {
1648 a = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(1, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1649 r = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(3, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1650 g = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(5, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1651 b = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(7, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1652 }
1653 else if (color.size() == 7)
1654 {
1655 r = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(1, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1656 g = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(3, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1657 b = (float) std::stoi(color.substr(5, 2), nullptr, 16) / 255;
1658 a = 1.f;
1659 }
1660 }
1661 else
1662 {
1663 if (color.size() == 9)
1664 {
1665 a = std::stoi(color.substr(1, 2), nullptr, 16);
1666 r = std::stoi(color.substr(3, 2), nullptr, 16);
1667 g = std::stoi(color.substr(5, 2), nullptr, 16);
1668 b = std::stoi(color.substr(7, 2), nullptr, 16);
1669 }
1670 else if (color.size() == 7)
1671 {
1672 r = std::stoi(color.substr(1, 2), nullptr, 16);
1673 g = std::stoi(color.substr(3, 2), nullptr, 16);
1674 b = std::stoi(color.substr(5, 2), nullptr, 16);
1675 a = 255;
1676 }
1677 }
1678 }
1679
1680 };
1681
1682 typedef Color<uint8_t> Colori;
1683 typedef Color<float> Colorf;
1684
1691 template<typename T>
1692 tson::Colorf Color<T>::asFloat()
1693 {
1694 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, float>::value)
1695 *this;
1696 else
1697 return tson::Colorf((float) r / 255, (float) g / 255, (float) b / 255, (float) a / 255);
1698 }
1699
1706 template<typename T>
1707 tson::Colori Color<T>::asInt()
1708 {
1709 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, float>::value)
1710 return tson::Colori(static_cast<std::uint8_t>((float) r * 255),
1711 static_cast<std::uint8_t>((float) g * 255),
1712 static_cast<std::uint8_t>((float) b * 255),
1713 static_cast<std::uint8_t>((float) a * 255));
1714 else
1715 *this;
1716 }
1717
1726 template<typename T>
1727 Color<T>::Color(T red, T green, T blue, T alpha)
1728 {
1729 r = red;
1730 g = green;
1731 b = blue;
1732 a = alpha;
1733 }
1734
1735 template<typename T>
1736 bool Color<T>::operator==(const std::string &rhs) const {
1737 Color other {rhs};
1738 return *this == other;
1739 }
1740
1741 template<typename T>
1742 bool Color<T>::operator==(const Color &rhs) const
1743 {
1744 return r == rhs.r &&
1745 g == rhs.g &&
1746 b == rhs.b &&
1747 a == rhs.a;
1748 }
1749
1750 template<typename T>
1751 bool Color<T>::operator!=(const Color &rhs) const
1752 {
1753 return !(rhs == *this);
1754 }
1755
1756}
1757
1758#endif //TILESON_COLOR_HPP
1759
1760/*** End of inlined file: Color.hpp ***/
1761
1762
1763/*** Start of inlined file: Vector2.hpp ***/
1764//
1765// Created by robin on 31.07.2019.
1766//
1767
1768#ifndef TILESON_VECTOR2_HPP
1769#define TILESON_VECTOR2_HPP
1770
1771namespace tson
1772{
1773 template<typename T>
1774 class Vector2
1775 {
1776
1777 public:
1778 inline Vector2(T xPos, T yPos);
1779 inline Vector2() { x = y = 0; }
1780
1781 inline bool operator==(const Vector2 &rhs) const;
1782 inline bool operator!=(const Vector2 &rhs) const;
1783
1784 T x;
1785 T y;
1786 };
1787
1794 template<typename T>
1795 Vector2<T>::Vector2(T xPos, T yPos)
1796 {
1797 x = xPos;
1798 y = yPos;
1799 }
1800
1801 template<typename T>
1802 bool Vector2<T>::operator==(const Vector2 &rhs) const
1803 {
1804 return x == rhs.x &&
1805 y == rhs.y;
1806 }
1807
1808 template<typename T>
1809 bool Vector2<T>::operator!=(const Vector2 &rhs) const
1810 {
1811 return !(rhs == *this);
1812 }
1813
1814 typedef Vector2<int> Vector2i;
1815 typedef Vector2<float> Vector2f;
1816}
1817
1818#endif //TILESON_VECTOR2_HPP
1819
1820/*** End of inlined file: Vector2.hpp ***/
1821
1822//#include "../external/json.hpp"
1823
1824/*** Start of inlined file: IJson.hpp ***/
1825//
1826// Created by robin on 06.01.2021.
1827//
1828
1829#ifndef TILESON_IJSON_HPP
1830#define TILESON_IJSON_HPP
1831
1832namespace tson
1833{
1834 class IJson
1835 {
1836 public:
1837
1838 virtual IJson& operator[](std::string_view key) = 0;
1839 virtual IJson &at(std::string_view key) = 0;
1840 virtual IJson &at(size_t pos) = 0;
1845 [[nodiscard]] virtual std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> array() = 0;
1846 [[nodiscard]] virtual std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> &array(std::string_view key) = 0;
1852 [[nodiscard]] virtual size_t size() const = 0;
1853 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool parse(const fs::path &path) = 0;
1854 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool parse(const void *data, size_t size) = 0;
1855
1856 template <typename T>
1857 [[nodiscard]] T get(std::string_view key);
1858 template <typename T>
1859 [[nodiscard]] T get();
1860 [[nodiscard]] virtual size_t count(std::string_view key) const = 0;
1861 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool any(std::string_view key) const = 0;
1862 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool isArray() const = 0;
1863 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool isObject() const = 0;
1864 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool isNull() const = 0;
1865
1871 [[nodiscard]] virtual fs::path directory() const = 0;
1872 virtual void directory(const fs::path &directory) = 0;
1873
1877 virtual ~IJson() = default;
1878
1879 protected:
1880 [[nodiscard]] virtual int32_t getInt32(std::string_view key) = 0;
1881 [[nodiscard]] virtual uint32_t getUInt32(std::string_view key) = 0;
1882 [[nodiscard]] virtual int64_t getInt64(std::string_view key) = 0;
1883 [[nodiscard]] virtual uint64_t getUInt64(std::string_view key) = 0;
1884 [[nodiscard]] virtual double getDouble(std::string_view key) = 0;
1885 [[nodiscard]] virtual float getFloat(std::string_view key) = 0;
1886 [[nodiscard]] virtual std::string getString(std::string_view key) = 0;
1887 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool getBool(std::string_view key) = 0;
1888
1889 [[nodiscard]] virtual int32_t getInt32() = 0;
1890 [[nodiscard]] virtual uint32_t getUInt32() = 0;
1891 [[nodiscard]] virtual int64_t getInt64() = 0;
1892 [[nodiscard]] virtual uint64_t getUInt64() = 0;
1893 [[nodiscard]] virtual double getDouble() = 0;
1894 [[nodiscard]] virtual float getFloat() = 0;
1895 [[nodiscard]] virtual std::string getString() = 0;
1896 [[nodiscard]] virtual bool getBool() = 0;
1897 };
1898
1899 template<typename T>
1900 T IJson::get(std::string_view key)
1901 {
1902 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, double>::value)
1903 return getDouble(key);
1904 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, float>::value)
1905 return getFloat(key);
1906 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, int32_t>::value)
1907 return getInt32(key);
1908 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, uint32_t>::value)
1909 return getUInt32(key);
1910 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, int64_t>::value)
1911 return getInt64(key);
1912 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, uint64_t>::value)
1913 return getUInt64(key);
1914 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, std::string>::value)
1915 return getString(key);
1916 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, bool>::value)
1917 return getBool(key);
1918 else
1919 return nullptr;
1920 }
1921
1922 template<typename T>
1923 T IJson::get()
1924 {
1925 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, double>::value)
1926 return getDouble();
1927 if constexpr (std::is_same<T, float>::value)
1928 return getFloat();
1929 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, int32_t>::value)
1930 return getInt32();
1931 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, uint32_t>::value)
1932 return getUInt32();
1933 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, int64_t>::value)
1934 return getInt64();
1935 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, uint64_t>::value)
1936 return getUInt64();
1937 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, std::string>::value)
1938 return getString();
1939 else if constexpr (std::is_same<T, bool>::value)
1940 return getBool();
1941 else
1942 return nullptr;
1943 }
1944
1945}
1946
1947#endif //TILESON_IJSON_HPP
1948
1949/*** End of inlined file: IJson.hpp ***/
1950
1951
1952
1953/*** Start of inlined file: NlohmannJson.hpp ***/
1954//
1955// Created by robin on 08.01.2021.
1956//
1957
1958#ifdef INCLUDE_NLOHMANN_JSON_HPP_
1959
1960#ifndef TILESON_NLOHMANNJSON_HPP
1961#define TILESON_NLOHMANNJSON_HPP
1962
1963namespace tson
1964{
1965 class NlohmannJson : public tson::IJson
1966 {
1967 public:
1968 inline NlohmannJson() = default;
1969
1970 IJson &operator[](std::string_view key) override
1971 {
1972 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
1973 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<NlohmannJson>(&m_json->operator[](key.data()));//.front());
1974
1975 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
1976 }
1977
1978 inline explicit NlohmannJson(nlohmann::json *json) : m_json {json}
1979 {
1980
1981 }
1982
1983 inline IJson& at(std::string_view key) override
1984 {
1985 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
1986 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<NlohmannJson>(&m_json->operator[](key.data()));//.front());
1987
1988 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
1989 }
1990
1991 inline IJson& at(size_t pos) override
1992 {
1993 if(m_arrayPosCache.count(pos) == 0)
1994 m_arrayPosCache[pos] = std::make_unique<NlohmannJson>(&m_json->at(pos));
1995
1996 return *m_arrayPosCache[pos];
1997 }
1998
1999 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> array() override
2000 {
2001 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> vec;
2002 for(auto &item : *m_json)
2003 {
2004 nlohmann::json *ptr = &item;
2005 vec.emplace_back(std::make_unique<NlohmannJson>(ptr));
2006 }
2007
2008 return vec;
2009 }
2010
2011 inline std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> &array(std::string_view key) override
2012 {
2013 if(m_arrayListDataCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2014 {
2015 if (m_json->count(key.data()) > 0 && m_json->operator[](key.data()).is_array())
2016 {
2017 std::for_each(m_json->operator[](key.data()).begin(), m_json->operator[](key.data()).end(), [&](nlohmann::json &item)
2018 {
2019 nlohmann::json *ptr = &item;
2020 m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()].emplace_back(std::make_unique<NlohmannJson>(ptr));
2021 });
2022 }
2023 }
2024
2025 return m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()];
2026 }
2027
2028 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t size() const override
2029 {
2030 return m_json->size();
2031 }
2032
2033 inline bool parse(const fs::path &path) override
2034 {
2035 clearCache();
2036 m_data = nullptr;
2037 m_json = nullptr;
2038 if (fs::exists(path) && fs::is_regular_file(path))
2039 {
2040 m_path = path.parent_path();
2041 m_data = std::make_unique<nlohmann::json>();
2042 std::ifstream i(path.generic_string());
2043 try
2044 {
2045 i >> *m_data;
2046 m_json = m_data.get();
2047 }
2048 catch (const nlohmann::json::parse_error &error)
2049 {
2050 std::string message = "Parse error: ";
2051 message += std::string(error.what());
2052 message += std::string("\n");
2053 std::cerr << message;
2054 return false;
2055 }
2056 return true;
2057 }
2058 return false;
2059 }
2060
2061 inline bool parse(const void *data, size_t size) override
2062 {
2063 clearCache();
2064 m_json = nullptr;
2065 m_data = std::make_unique<nlohmann::json>();
2066 tson::MemoryStream mem{(uint8_t *) data, size};
2067 try
2068 {
2069 mem >> *m_data;
2070 m_json = m_data.get();
2071 }
2072 catch (const nlohmann::json::parse_error &error)
2073 {
2074 std::string message = "Parse error: ";
2075 message += std::string(error.what());
2076 message += std::string("\n");
2077 std::cerr << message;
2078 return false;
2079 }
2080 return true;
2081 }
2082
2083 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t count(std::string_view key) const override
2084 {
2085 return m_json->count(key);
2086 }
2087
2088 [[nodiscard]] inline bool any(std::string_view key) const override
2089 {
2090 return count(key) > 0;
2091 }
2092
2093 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isArray() const override
2094 {
2095 return m_json->is_array();
2096 }
2097
2098 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isObject() const override
2099 {
2100 return m_json->is_object();
2101 }
2102
2103 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isNull() const override
2104 {
2105 return m_json->is_null();
2106 }
2107
2108 fs::path directory() const override
2109 {
2110 return m_path;
2111 }
2112
2113 void directory(const fs::path &directory) override
2114 {
2115 m_path = directory;
2116 }
2117
2118 protected:
2119 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32(std::string_view key) override
2120 {
2121 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<int32_t>();
2122 }
2123
2124 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32(std::string_view key) override
2125 {
2126 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<uint32_t>();
2127 }
2128
2129 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64(std::string_view key) override
2130 {
2131 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<int64_t>();
2132 }
2133
2134 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64(std::string_view key) override
2135 {
2136 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<uint64_t>();
2137 }
2138
2139 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble(std::string_view key) override
2140 {
2141 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<double>();
2142 }
2143
2144 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString(std::string_view key) override
2145 {
2146 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<std::string>();
2147 }
2148
2149 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool(std::string_view key) override
2150 {
2151 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<bool>();
2152 }
2153
2154 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat(std::string_view key) override
2155 {
2156 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).get<float>();
2157 }
2158
2159 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32() override
2160 {
2161 return m_json->get<int32_t>();
2162 }
2163
2164 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32() override
2165 {
2166 return m_json->get<uint32_t>();
2167 }
2168
2169 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64() override
2170 {
2171 return m_json->get<int64_t>();
2172 }
2173
2174 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64() override
2175 {
2176 return m_json->get<uint64_t>();
2177 }
2178
2179 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble() override
2180 {
2181 return m_json->get<double>();
2182 }
2183
2184 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString() override
2185 {
2186 return m_json->get<std::string>();
2187 }
2188
2189 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool() override
2190 {
2191 return m_json->get<bool>();
2192 }
2193
2194 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat() override
2195 {
2196 return m_json->get<float>();
2197 }
2198
2199 private:
2200 inline void clearCache()
2201 {
2202 m_arrayCache.clear();
2203 m_arrayPosCache.clear();
2204 m_arrayListDataCache.clear();
2205 }
2206
2207 nlohmann::json *m_json = nullptr;
2208 std::unique_ptr<nlohmann::json> m_data = nullptr; //Only used if this is the owner json!
2209 fs::path m_path;
2210
2211 //Cache!
2212 std::map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayCache;
2213 std::map<size_t, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayPosCache;
2214 std::map<std::string, std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>>> m_arrayListDataCache;
2215
2216 };
2217}
2218#endif //TILESON_NLOHMANNJSON_HPP
2219
2220#endif //INCLUDE_NLOHMANN_JSON_HPP_
2221/*** End of inlined file: NlohmannJson.hpp ***/
2222
2223
2224/*** Start of inlined file: PicoJson.hpp ***/
2225//
2226// Created by robin on 11.01.2021.
2227//
2228
2229#ifdef picojson_h
2230#ifndef TILESON_PICOJSON_HPP
2231#define TILESON_PICOJSON_HPP
2232
2233namespace tson
2234{
2235 class PicoJson : public tson::IJson
2236 {
2237 public:
2238 inline PicoJson() = default;
2239
2240 IJson &operator[](std::string_view key) override
2241 {
2242 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2243 {
2244 if(m_json->is<picojson::object>())
2245 {
2246 picojson::object &o = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2247 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<PicoJson>(&o[key.data()]);
2248 }
2249 }
2250
2251 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
2252 }
2253
2254 inline explicit PicoJson(picojson::value *json) : m_json {json}
2255 {
2256
2257 }
2258
2259 inline IJson& at(std::string_view key) override
2260 {
2261 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2262 {
2263 if(m_json->is<picojson::object>())
2264 {
2265 picojson::object &o = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2266 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<PicoJson>(&o[key.data()]);
2267 }
2268 }
2269 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
2270 }
2271
2272 inline IJson& at(size_t pos) override
2273 {
2274 if(m_arrayPosCache.count(pos) == 0)
2275 {
2276 picojson::array &a = m_json->get<picojson::array>();
2277 m_arrayPosCache[pos] = std::make_unique<PicoJson>(&a.at(pos));
2278 }
2279
2280 return *m_arrayPosCache[pos];
2281 }
2282
2283 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> array() override
2284 {
2285 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> vec;
2286 if(m_json->is<picojson::array>())
2287 {
2288 picojson::array &a = m_json->get<picojson::array>();
2289 for (auto &item : a)
2290 {
2291 picojson::value *ptr = &item;
2292 vec.emplace_back(std::make_unique<PicoJson>(ptr));
2293 }
2294 }
2295
2296 return vec;
2297 }
2298
2299 inline std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> &array(std::string_view key) override
2300 {
2301 if(m_arrayListDataCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2302 {
2303 if(count(key.data()) > 0)
2304 {
2305 if (isObject())
2306 {
2307 picojson::object &obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2308 picojson::value &v = obj.at(key.data());
2309 bool isArray = v.is<picojson::array>();
2310 if (isArray)
2311 {
2312 picojson::array &a = v.get<picojson::array>();
2313
2314 std::for_each(a.begin(), a.end(), [&](picojson::value &item)
2315 {
2316 picojson::value *ptr = &item;
2317 m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()].emplace_back(std::make_unique<PicoJson>(ptr));
2318 });
2319 }
2320 }
2321 }
2322 }
2323
2324 return m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()];
2325 }
2326
2327 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t size() const override
2328 {
2329 if (m_json->is<picojson::object>())
2330 {
2331 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2332 return obj.size();
2333 }
2334 return 0;
2335 }
2336
2337 inline bool parse(const fs::path &path) override
2338 {
2339 clearCache();
2340 m_data = nullptr;
2341 m_json = nullptr;
2342 if (fs::exists(path) && fs::is_regular_file(path))
2343 {
2344 m_path = path.parent_path();
2345 m_data = std::make_unique<picojson::value>();
2346 std::ifstream i(path.generic_string());
2347 try
2348 {
2349 std::string error = picojson::parse(*m_data, i);
2350 if(!error.empty())
2351 {
2352 std::cerr << "PicoJson parse error: " << error << "\n";
2353 return false;
2354 }
2355 //i >> *m_data;
2356 m_json = m_data.get();
2357 }
2358 catch (const std::exception &error)
2359 {
2360 std::string message = "Parse error: ";
2361 message += std::string(error.what());
2362 message += std::string("\n");
2363 std::cerr << message;
2364 return false;
2365 }
2366 return true;
2367 }
2368 return false;
2369 }
2370
2371 inline bool parse(const void *data, size_t size) override
2372 {
2373 clearCache();
2374 m_json = nullptr;
2375 m_data = std::make_unique<picojson::value>();
2376 tson::MemoryStream mem{(uint8_t *) data, size};
2377 try
2378 {
2379 std::string error = picojson::parse(*m_data, mem);
2380 if(!error.empty())
2381 {
2382 std::cerr << "PicoJson parse error: " << error << "\n";
2383 return false;
2384 }
2385 //mem >> *m_data;
2386 m_json = m_data.get();
2387 }
2388 catch (const std::exception &error)
2389 {
2390 std::string message = "Parse error: ";
2391 message += std::string(error.what());
2392 message += std::string("\n");
2393 std::cerr << message;
2394 return false;
2395 }
2396 return true;
2397 }
2398
2399 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t count(std::string_view key) const override
2400 {
2401 if (isObject())
2402 {
2403 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2404 return obj.count(key.data());
2405 }
2406
2407 return m_json->contains(key.data()) ? 1 : 0;
2408 }
2409
2410 [[nodiscard]] inline bool any(std::string_view key) const override
2411 {
2412 return count(key) > 0;
2413 }
2414
2415 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isArray() const override
2416 {
2417 return m_json->is<picojson::array>();
2418 }
2419
2420 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isObject() const override
2421 {
2422 return m_json->is<picojson::object>();
2423 }
2424
2425 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isNull() const override
2426 {
2427 return m_json->is<picojson::null>();
2428 }
2429
2430 fs::path directory() const override
2431 {
2432 return m_path;
2433 }
2434
2435 void directory(const fs::path &directory) override
2436 {
2437 m_path = directory;
2438 }
2439
2440 protected:
2441 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32(std::string_view key) override
2442 {
2443 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2444 return static_cast<int32_t>(getDouble(key));
2445 }
2446
2447 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32(std::string_view key) override
2448 {
2449 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2450 return static_cast<uint32_t>(getDouble(key));
2451 }
2452
2453 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64(std::string_view key) override
2454 {
2455 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2456 return static_cast<int64_t>(getDouble(key));
2457 }
2458
2459 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64(std::string_view key) override
2460 {
2461 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2462 return static_cast<uint64_t>(getDouble(key));
2463 }
2464
2465 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble(std::string_view key) override
2466 {
2467 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2468 return obj[key.data()].get<double>();
2469 }
2470
2471 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString(std::string_view key) override
2472 {
2473 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2474 return obj[key.data()].get<std::string>();
2475 }
2476
2477 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool(std::string_view key) override
2478 {
2479 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2480 return obj[key.data()].get<bool>();
2481 }
2482
2483 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat(std::string_view key) override
2484 {
2485 picojson::object obj = m_json->get<picojson::object>();
2486 return static_cast<float>(getDouble(key));
2487 }
2488
2489 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32() override
2490 {
2491 return static_cast<int32_t>(getDouble());
2492 }
2493
2494 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32() override
2495 {
2496 return static_cast<uint32_t>(getDouble());
2497 }
2498
2499 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64() override
2500 {
2501 return static_cast<int64_t>(getDouble());
2502 }
2503
2504 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64() override
2505 {
2506 return static_cast<uint64_t>(getDouble());
2507 }
2508
2509 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble() override
2510 {
2511 return m_json->get<double>();
2512 }
2513
2514 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString() override
2515 {
2516 return m_json->get<std::string>();
2517 }
2518
2519 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool() override
2520 {
2521 return m_json->get<bool>();
2522 }
2523
2524 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat() override
2525 {
2526 return static_cast<float>(getDouble());
2527 }
2528
2529 private:
2530 inline void clearCache()
2531 {
2532 m_arrayCache.clear();
2533 m_arrayPosCache.clear();
2534 m_arrayListDataCache.clear();
2535 }
2536
2537 picojson::value *m_json = nullptr;
2538 std::unique_ptr<picojson::value> m_data = nullptr; //Only used if this is the owner json!
2539 fs::path m_path;
2540
2541 //Cache!
2542 std::map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayCache;
2543 std::map<size_t, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayPosCache;
2544 std::map<std::string, std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>>> m_arrayListDataCache;
2545
2546 };
2547}
2548#endif //TILESON_PICOJSON_HPP
2549#endif

2555/*** Start of inlined file: Json11.hpp ***/
2556//
2557// Created by robin on 16.01.2021.
2558//
2559
2560#ifndef TILESON_JSON11_HPP
2561#define TILESON_JSON11_HPP
2562
2563namespace tson
2564{
2565 class Json11 : public tson::IJson
2566 {
2567 public:
2568 inline Json11() = default;
2569
2570 IJson &operator[](std::string_view key) override
2571 {
2572 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2573 {
2574 if(m_json->is_object())
2575 {
2576 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<Json11>(m_json->operator[](key.data()));
2577 }
2578 }
2579
2580 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
2581 }
2582
2583 inline explicit Json11(const json11::Json &json) : m_json {&json}
2584 {
2585
2586 }
2587
2588 inline IJson& at(std::string_view key) override
2589 {
2590 if(m_arrayCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2591 {
2592 if(m_json->is_object())
2593 {
2594 m_arrayCache[key.data()] = std::make_unique<Json11>(m_json->operator[](key.data()));
2595 }
2596 }
2597 return *m_arrayCache[key.data()].get();
2598 }
2599
2600 inline IJson& at(size_t pos) override
2601 {
2602 if(m_arrayPosCache.count(pos) == 0)
2603 {
2604 const std::vector<json11::Json> &a = m_json->array_items();
2605 m_arrayPosCache[pos] = std::make_unique<Json11>(a.at(pos));
2606 }
2607
2608 return *m_arrayPosCache[pos];
2609 }
2610
2611 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> array() override
2612 {
2613 std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> vec;
2614 if(m_json->is_array())
2615 {
2616 for (const json11::Json &item : m_json->array_items())
2617 {
2618 vec.emplace_back(std::make_unique<Json11>(item));
2619 }
2620 }
2621
2622 return vec;
2623 }
2624
2625 inline std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>> &array(std::string_view key) override
2626 {
2627 if(m_arrayListDataCache.count(key.data()) == 0)
2628 {
2629 if(count(key.data()) > 0)
2630 {
2631 if(isObject())
2632 {
2633 const json11::Json &v = m_json->operator[](key.data());
2634 if(v.is_array())
2635 {
2636 for (const json11::Json &item : v.array_items())
2637 {
2638 m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()].emplace_back(std::make_unique<Json11>(item));
2639 }
2640 }
2641 }
2642 }
2643 }
2644
2645 return m_arrayListDataCache[key.data()];
2646 }
2647
2648 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t size() const override
2649 {
2650 if(m_json->is_object())
2651 return m_json->object_items().size();
2652 else if(m_json->is_array())
2653 return m_json->array_items().size();
2654
2655 return 0;
2656 }
2657
2658 inline bool parse(const fs::path &path) override
2659 {
2660 clearCache();
2661 m_data = nullptr;
2662 m_json = nullptr;
2663 if (fs::exists(path) && fs::is_regular_file(path))
2664 {
2665 std::ifstream file(path.generic_string());
2666 std::string str;
2667 m_path = path.parent_path();
2668
2669 file.seekg(0, std::ios::end);
2670 str.reserve(file.tellg());
2671 file.seekg(0, std::ios::beg);
2672
2673 str.assign((std::istreambuf_iterator<char>(file)),
2674 std::istreambuf_iterator<char>());
2675
2676 m_data = std::make_unique<json11::Json>();
2677
2678 try
2679 {
2680 std::string strError;
2681 *m_data = json11::Json::parse(str, strError);
2682 if(!strError.empty())
2683 {
2684 std::cerr << strError << "\n";
2685 return false;
2686 }
2687 m_json = m_data.get();
2688 }
2689 catch (const std::exception &error)
2690 {
2691 std::string message = "Json11 parse error: ";
2692 message += std::string(error.what());
2693 message += std::string("\n");
2694 std::cerr << message;
2695 return false;
2696 }
2697 return true;
2698 }
2699 return false;
2700 }
2701
2702 inline bool parse(const void *data, size_t size) override
2703 {
2704 clearCache();
2705 m_json = nullptr;
2706 std::string str;
2707
2708 str.reserve(size);
2709
2710 tson::MemoryStream mem{(uint8_t *) data, size};
2711
2712 str.assign((std::istreambuf_iterator<char>(mem)),
2713 std::istreambuf_iterator<char>());
2714
2715 m_data = std::make_unique<json11::Json>();
2716
2717 try
2718 {
2719 std::string strError;
2720
2721 *m_data = json11::Json::parse(str, strError);
2722 if(!strError.empty())
2723 {
2724 std::cout << strError << "\n";
2725 return false;
2726 }
2727 m_json = m_data.get();
2728 }
2729 catch (const std::exception &error)
2730 {
2731 std::string message = "Json11 parse error: ";
2732 message += std::string(error.what());
2733 message += std::string("\n");
2734 std::cerr << message;
2735 return false;
2736 }
2737 return true;
2738 }
2739
2740 [[nodiscard]] inline size_t count(std::string_view key) const override
2741 {
2742 if (isObject())
2743 {
2744 //const json11::Json &j = m_json->operator[](key.data());
2745 //size_t s1 = j.object_items().size();
2746 return m_json->object_items().count(key.data());
2747 }
2748
2749 return 0;
2750 }
2751
2752 [[nodiscard]] inline bool any(std::string_view key) const override
2753 {
2754 return count(key) > 0;
2755 }
2756
2757 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isArray() const override
2758 {
2759 return m_json->is_array();
2760 }
2761
2762 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isObject() const override
2763 {
2764 return m_json->is_object();
2765 }
2766
2767 [[nodiscard]] inline bool isNull() const override
2768 {
2769 return m_json->is_null();
2770 }
2771
2772 fs::path directory() const override
2773 {
2774 return m_path;
2775 }
2776
2777 void directory(const fs::path &directory) override
2778 {
2779 m_path = directory;
2780 }
2781
2782 protected:
2783 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32(std::string_view key) override
2784 {
2785 return static_cast<int32_t>(getDouble(key));
2786 }
2787
2788 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32(std::string_view key) override
2789 {
2790 return static_cast<uint32_t>(getDouble(key));
2791 }
2792
2793 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64(std::string_view key) override
2794 {
2795 return static_cast<int64_t>(getDouble(key));
2796 }
2797
2798 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64(std::string_view key) override
2799 {
2800 return static_cast<uint64_t>(getDouble(key));
2801 }
2802
2803 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble(std::string_view key) override
2804 {
2805 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).number_value();
2806 }
2807
2808 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString(std::string_view key) override
2809 {
2810 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).string_value(); // .get<std::string>();
2811 }
2812
2813 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool(std::string_view key) override
2814 {
2815 return m_json->operator[](key.data()).bool_value();
2816 }
2817
2818 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat(std::string_view key) override
2819 {
2820 return static_cast<float>(getDouble(key));
2821 }
2822
2823 [[nodiscard]] inline int32_t getInt32() override
2824 {
2825 return static_cast<int32_t>(getDouble());
2826 }
2827
2828 [[nodiscard]] inline uint32_t getUInt32() override
2829 {
2830 return static_cast<uint32_t>(getDouble());
2831 }
2832
2833 [[nodiscard]] inline int64_t getInt64() override
2834 {
2835 return static_cast<int64_t>(getDouble());
2836 }
2837
2838 [[nodiscard]] inline uint64_t getUInt64() override
2839 {
2840 return static_cast<uint64_t>(getDouble());
2841 }
2842
2843 [[nodiscard]] inline double getDouble() override
2844 {
2845 return m_json->number_value();
2846 }
2847
2848 [[nodiscard]] inline std::string getString() override
2849 {
2850 return m_json->string_value();
2851 }
2852
2853 [[nodiscard]] inline bool getBool() override
2854 {
2855 return m_json->bool_value();
2856 }
2857
2858 [[nodiscard]] float getFloat() override
2859 {
2860 return static_cast<float>(getDouble());
2861 }
2862
2863 private:
2864
2865 inline void clearCache()
2866 {
2867 m_arrayCache.clear();
2868 m_arrayPosCache.clear();
2869 m_arrayListDataCache.clear();
2870 }
2871
2872 //Owner values
2873 char *m_endptr;
2874 std::unique_ptr<json11::Json> m_data = nullptr; //Only used if this is the owner json!
2875
2876 const json11::Json *m_json = nullptr;
2877 fs::path m_path;
2878
2879 //Cache!
2880 std::map<std::string, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayCache;
2881 std::map<size_t, std::unique_ptr<IJson>> m_arrayPosCache;
2882 std::map<std::string, std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IJson>>> m_arrayListDataCache;
2883
2884 };
2885}
2886
2887#endif //TILESON_JSON11_HPP
2888

2905/*** Start of inlined file: Chunk.hpp ***/
2906//
2907// Created by robin on 22.03.2020.
2908//
2909
2910#ifndef TILESON_CHUNK_HPP
2911#define TILESON_CHUNK_HPP
2912
2913//#include "../external/json.hpp"
2914
2915namespace tson
2916{
2917 class Chunk
2918 {
2919 public:
2920 inline Chunk() = default;
2921 inline explicit Chunk(IJson &json);
2922 inline bool parse(IJson &json);
2923
2924 [[nodiscard]] inline const std::vector<int> &getData() const;
2925 [[nodiscard]] inline const std::string &getBase64Data() const;
2926 [[nodiscard]] inline const Vector2i &getSize() const;
2927 [[nodiscard]] inline const Vector2i &getPosition() const;
2928
2929 private:
2930 std::vector<int> m_data;
2931 std::string m_base64Data;
2932 tson::Vector2i m_size;
2933 tson::Vector2i m_position;
2934 };
2935}
2936
2937#endif //TILESON_CHUNK_HPP
2938
2943tson::Chunk::Chunk(IJson &json)
2944{
2945 parse(json);
2946}
2947
2953bool tson::Chunk::parse(IJson &json)
2954{
2955 bool allFound = true;
2956
2957 if(json.count("width") > 0 && json.count("height") > 0)
2958 m_size = {json["width"].get<int>(), json["height"].get<int>()}; else allFound = false;
2959 if(json.count("x") > 0 && json.count("y") > 0)
2960 m_position = {json["x"].get<int>(), json["y"].get<int>()}; else allFound = false;
2961
2962 //Handle DATA (Optional)
2963 if(json.count("data") > 0)
2964 {
2965 if(json["data"].isArray())
2966 {
2967 auto &data = json.array("data");
2968 std::for_each(data.begin(), data.end(), [&](std::unique_ptr<IJson> &item) { m_data.push_back(item->get<int>()); });
2969 }
2970 else
2971 m_base64Data = json["data"].get<std::string>();
2972 }
2973
2974 return allFound;
2975}
2976
2981const std::vector<int> &tson::Chunk::getData() const
2982{
2983 return m_data;
2984}
2985
2990const std::string &tson::Chunk::getBase64Data() const
2991{
2992 return m_base64Data;
2993}
2994
2999const tson::Vector2i &tson::Chunk::getSize() const
3000{
3001 return m_size;
3002}
3003
3008const tson::Vector2i &tson::Chunk::getPosition() const
3009{
3010 return m_position;
3011}
3012/*** End of inlined file: Chunk.hpp ***/

3033

3035/*** Start of inlined file: Property.hpp ***/

3040#ifndef TILESON_PROPERTY_HPP
3041#define TILESON_PROPERTY_HPP
3042
3043//#include "../../TilesonConfig.h"

3046
